questions.question: A substance 'X' is used in white washing. When 'X' reacts with water, it produces 'Y' with the liberation of heat. 'Y' reacts with a gas 'Z' to form a thin white layer on walls after 2-3 days. Identify X, Y, and Z and write all balanced equations.
questions.answer: 'X' is Calcium Oxide (CaO). 'Y' is Calcium Hydroxide [Ca(OH)2]. 'Z' is Carbon Dioxide (CO2). Equations: 1. CaO + H2O → Ca(OH)2 + Heat. 2. Ca(OH)2 + CO2 → CaCO3 + H2O. The thin layer is Calcium Carbonate.

questions.question: What is observed when lead nitrate powder is heated in a boiling tube? Write a balanced chemical equation and identify the type of reaction and the gases evolved.
questions.answer: Observations: Emission of brown fumes and a yellow residue remains. The brown fumes are Nitrogen Dioxide (NO2) and the residue is Lead Oxide (PbO). Equation: 2Pb(NO3)2(s) → 2PbO(s) + 4NO2(g) + O2(g). This is a Thermal Decomposition reaction.

questions.question: Explain the electrolysis of water with a neat diagrammatic description. Why is the volume of gas collected at one electrode double the other?
questions.answer: During electrolysis, water decomposes into Hydrogen and Oxygen. At the cathode, H+ ions gain electrons to form H2 gas. At the anode, O2 gas is formed. Water (H2O) contains two parts hydrogen for every one part oxygen by molar ratio, so the volume of Hydrogen is double that of Oxygen.
answer_outline: Stoichiometric ratio of H2 to O2 (2:1)

questions.question: Explain the significance of the 'Reactivity Series' in displacement reactions. Can a reaction occur between Copper and Zinc Sulphate? Justify.
questions.answer: The reactivity series ranks metals by their strength as reducing agents. A metal higher in the series can displace one lower. Copper cannot react with Zinc Sulphate because Copper is less reactive than Zinc. No reaction occurs as Copper cannot displace Zinc from its salt.
answer_outline: Comparison of Cu and Zn reactivity

questions.question: In the reaction: $MnO_2 + 4HCl ightarrow MnCl_2 + 2H_2O + Cl_2$, identify the species being oxidized and reduced. Justify based on the electronic concept or oxygen/hydrogen transfer.
questions.answer: HCl is oxidized to Cl2 because it loses hydrogen. MnO2 is reduced to MnCl2 because it loses oxygen. MnO2 acts as the oxidizing agent, and HCl acts as the reducing agent.

questions.question: Why is the decomposition of Ferrous Sulphate crystals considered a complex decomposition? Write the equation and state the characteristic odor of the gases.
questions.answer: It is complex because it yields three products: a solid (Fe2O3) and two gases (SO2, SO3). Equation: 2FeSO4(s) → Fe2O3(s) + SO2(g) + SO3(g). The gases have a characteristic choking smell of burning sulfur.
answer_outline: Observation (color change from green to reddish-brown)

questions.question: What is the difference between Corrosion and Rusting? Discuss three methods to prevent the corrosion of iron in industrial applications.
questions.answer: Corrosion is the general degradation of any metal by environmental agents; Rusting is specifically the corrosion of iron forming hydrated ferric oxide. Prevention: 1. Galvanization (coating with Zinc). 2. Painting/Oiling. 3. Alloying (making Stainless Steel).

questions.question: A silver ornament becomes black when kept in the open for a few days. Name the phenomenon and the black substance formed. How can this be reversed or prevented?
questions.answer: The phenomenon is 'Tarnishing' (a form of corrosion). Silver reacts with Hydrogen Sulphide (H2S) in the air to form black Silver Sulphide (Ag2S). It can be prevented by storing in airtight containers or using anti-tarnish strips.

questions.question: Explain why a combustion reaction is always a redox reaction. Support your answer with the example of burning methane.
questions.answer: Combustion involves a substance reacting with oxygen. In the process, the fuel is oxidized (gains oxygen/loses hydrogen) and oxygen is reduced. Example: CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O. Here, C in CH4 is oxidized and O2 is reduced.
answer_outline: Role of Oxygen as oxidizing agent

questions.question: Predict the products and write the balanced chemical equation for the reaction between Barium Chloride and Aluminium Sulphate. State the type of reaction.
questions.answer: This is a Double Displacement reaction. The products are Barium Sulphate (precipitate) and Aluminium Chloride. Balanced Equation: 3BaCl2(aq) + Al2(SO4)3(aq) → 3BaSO4(s) + 2AlCl3(aq).
